formulas.name: CIA Triad
formulas.variables: Three key aspects of network security
formulas.notes: The CIA triad is a widely accepted model that describes the fundamental principles of network security, ensuring that data is kept confidential, its integrity is maintained, and it remains available when needed.

formulas.name: Masquerade Attack
formulas.formula: An attacker posing as a trusted entity to gain unauthorized access
formulas.notes: A masquerade attack involves an attacker disguising themselves as a trusted entity to deceive users and gain unauthorized access to a network or system.

formulas.name: Man-in-the-Middle (MitM) Attack
formulas.formula: An attacker intercepting communication between two parties
formulas.notes: A Man-in-the-Middle (MitM) attack occurs when an attacker intercepts communication between two parties, often to steal sensitive information or inject malware.

formulas.name: Secure Sockets Layer (SSL)/Transport Layer Security (TLS)
formulas.formula: A protocol for secure communication over the internet
formulas.notes: SSL/TLS is a cryptographic protocol that provides secure communication over the internet by encrypting data in transit and authenticating the identity of the communicating parties.

formulas.name: Secure Shell (SSH)
formulas.formula: A protocol for secure remote access to a network or system
formulas.notes: SSH is a protocol that provides secure remote access to a network or system by encrypting data in transit and authenticating the identity of the user.

formulas.name: Firewall Rule
formulas.formula: Allow or block network traffic based on rules
formulas.notes: A firewall rule specifies whether to allow or block network traffic based on criteria such as source IP address, destination IP address, and port numbers.

formulas.name: Access Control List (ACL)
formulas.formula: A list of rules governing access to a network or system
formulas.notes: An ACL is a list of rules that govern access to a network or system, specifying which users or groups are allowed to access certain resources or perform specific actions.

formulas.name: Least Privilege Principle
formulas.formula: Granting users the minimum privileges necessary to perform their tasks
formulas.notes: The least privilege principle states that users should be granted only the minimum privileges necessary to perform their tasks, reducing the risk of unauthorized access or malicious activity.
